tips.summary: Start IdeaVim Tutor from the status-bar Vim icon
details: Click the bottom-right Vim indicator in the IDE status bar
Choose Tutor from that menu

tips.summary: Map CapsLock as a dual-role Esc/Ctrl key
details: Tap for Esc, hold for Ctrl
Cuts hand movement for Vim commands
Use Karabiner, keyd, Kanata or similar
Use :set paragraph-motion
{ and } stop treating every blank line as a hard break
Useful in code with indented blank separators
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

tips.summary: Extend % to tags and control blocks
details: Use :set matchit
Use % on if/else/end or HTML/XML tags
Extends % beyond (), {}, and []

tips.summary: Jump by two chars with sneak
details: Use :set sneak
sfo jumps to the next fo; Sfo searches backward
Great for fast local jumps without /
Shows codepoint and representation
Great for weird whitespace issues
tips.review_note: Generated command map includes ga, but runtime test did not show visible output in IdeaVim.

details: Use :set highlightedyank
yy, yiw, and other yanks briefly highlight the copy
Helpful when you want visual confirmation
"0 holds the most recent yank
Paste it with "0p even after deletes
tips.review_note: Skipped as a standalone tip because the inside-vs-around idea is better folded into the concrete text-object tips.

details: "1 usually holds the most recent delete
Paste with "1p after an accidental dd
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: "ayiw yanks a word into register a
Paste later with "ap
Keeps your unnamed register free for newer yanks
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: Use :set ReplaceWithRegister
gr{motion} replaces text with the current register
"agrw uses register a for the replacement

tips.summary: Append to a named register with "A
details: "A appends into register a instead of replacing it
Use after "ayy or "ayiw to collect snippets
Paste the combined text later with "ap
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: "_d deletes without overwriting your registers
Use before pasting repeatedly
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: "- stores short deletes smaller than one line
Useful after x, dw, or other small cuts
Paste it with "-p
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: Select then use "_dP
Keeps your previous yank intact
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: :reg shows registers and macros
Useful when you forgot what you yanked
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: :put a inserts register a below the cursor
:put! a inserts it above the cursor
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: "=2+2<CR>p inserts result
Also works in Insert with Ctrl-r =
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too register-specific for the trimmed main set.

details: x deletes char under cursor, p puts it after
Fast fix for adjacent typos
gqap formats a paragraph
Uses textwidth when set
:set textwidth=80 for docs/comments
gq uses textwidth for formatting
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is a lower-priority options/config tip.

details: gqap formats current paragraph
Set textwidth for consistent wrapping
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on lower-priority textwidth/formatting configuration context.

tips.summary: Comment code with gcc and gc motions
details: Use :set commentary
gcc comments a line; gcap comments a paragraph
gc in Visual comments the selection

tips.summary: Add or change pairs with surround
details: Use :set surround
ysiw) adds (); cs"' swaps quote types
ds" removes surrounding quotes
Use :set exchange
cxiw marks text; repeat on a second target to swap
Use cxx to exchange whole lines
:3,10d deletes lines 3–10
Use '<,'> after Visual selection too
:3,10y yanks lines 3–10
Great for copying blocks precisely
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is too mechanical and weaker than the broader :t {address} copy tip.

details: Try :g/pat/p to preview matches
Then :g/pat/d to delete safely
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it is a narrower follow-up pattern and less valuable than the broader :g substitute tip.

tips.summary: Text objects: inside vs around i / a
details: i{obj} excludes delimiters, a{obj} includes them
Works with d/c/y/v
tips.review_note: Skipped as a standalone tip because the inside-vs-around idea is already folded into the concrete text-object tips.

details: ci' changes inside single quotes
Great for quick string edits
tips.review_note: Skipped as a duplicate variant of the double-quote text-object tip.

details: ci` changes inside backticks
Handy in Markdown and JS templates
tips.review_note: Skipped as another delimiter variant; v2 keeps the clearer double-quote example instead.

details: Text objects are edit units
Use i for inside and a for around
Combine with d, c, y, or v like ciw
tips.review_note: Skipped as a standalone theory tip because v2 keeps concrete text-object examples instead.

details: ci[ changes inside []
Good for array/index edits
tips.review_note: Skipped as a duplicate pair-delimiter variant; parentheses are the representative pair example in v2.

tips.summary: Edit inside braces ci{ and ciB
details: ci{ changes inside {}
ciB is an alias for braces in many setups
tips.review_note: Skipped as a duplicate pair-delimiter variant; parentheses are the representative pair example in v2.

tips.summary: Select inside/around pairs vi( and va{
details: vi( selects inside parentheses
va{ selects including braces
tips.review_note: Skipped because the visual pair-selection pattern is too reference-like for the trimmed set.

details: vip selects inner paragraph
vap includes surrounding blank lines
tips.review_note: Skipped as a standalone tip because paragraph around-selection is folded into the existing viw/vip visual tip.

details: diw deletes inner word
daw deletes word + surrounding space
tips.review_note: Skipped because v2 already keeps the stronger concrete word tips for delete, change, yank, and select.

details: Use :set argtextobj
cia changes one argument; daa deletes one
Great for calls with nested parentheses
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

tips.summary: Select the whole file with ae / ie
details: Use :set textobj-entire
vae or vie selects the whole buffer
Useful when ggVG feels clumsy
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

tips.summary: Target an indent block with ii / ai
details: Use :set indent-object
vai selects the current indent block; dii deletes it
Useful in Python and nested code
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

details: is selects the inner sentence
as includes trailing space
Works with c, d, and y like cis or das
tips.review_note: Skipped because sentence text objects are more niche than the stronger word, quote, pair, and paragraph examples.

details: cit changes inside an HTML/XML tag
dat deletes the tag and its content
Requires tag text-object support in your setup
tips.review_note: Skipped because support is setup-dependent and the tip set is avoiding ambiguous or plugin-like text-object variants for now.

details: vit selects text inside a tag
vat selects the tag and its text
tips.review_note: Skipped because tag text-object support is setup-dependent and not part of the trimmed core set.

tips.summary: Open the project tree with NERDTree
details: Use :set NERDTree
:NERDTreeToggle opens the file tree
:NERDTreeFind reveals the current file
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

details: ma sets mark a in this file
Use mA for a global mark; ideamarks can sync A-Z to IDE bookmarks
tips.review_note: Skipped because the workflow-oriented mark tip is stronger and this one is too reference-like.

details: Ctrl-w + and - adjust height
Ctrl-w < and > adjust width
Remap if your IDE keymap intercepts them
:tabnew opens a fresh Vim tab page
Useful when you want a separate split layout
tips.review_note: Skipped because it is too reference-like and weaker than stronger tab-use workflow tips.

details: :tabs shows all open tab pages
Use it when you need an overview before switching

tips.summary: Use gi with g; / g, for edit loops
details: gi returns to the last insert position
g; and g, walk older and newer changes
Useful for repeated small edits
:&& repeats the last :s and keeps flags
Use it on a new range or Visual selection
:%&& reapplies it file-wide
:read! {cmd} inserts command output below cursor
Example: :read! git branch --show-current
Great for notes, snippets, and diagnostics
Use if has('ide') ... endif in shared vimrc files
Keep <Action>(...) maps and IdeaVim options inside
Lets Vim or Neovim keep separate fallbacks
:source ~/.ideavimrc after config edits
Plugin setup docs recommend this after new set commands
Faster than restarting the IDE

tips.summary: Reuse your Vim config with source ~/.vimrc
details: Put source ~/.vimrc in ~/.ideavimrc
Keep shared motions and mappings in one place
Wrap IDE-only parts with if has('ide')
Use :set functiontextobj
vif selects inner function; yaf yanks a function
Useful for large methods and quick refactors
tips.review_note: Skipped for now because it depends on a plugin-backed text object.

details: Use :set multiple-cursors
Map <C-n> to <Plug>NextWholeOccurrence first
Press <C-n> repeatedly to add the next match
Use :set switch
Use gs on true/false, &&/||, or similar pairs
Great for fast boolean and operator flips
In Insert mode, Ctrl-n picks the next word match
Ctrl-p picks the previous one
Good for repeating names already in the file
